summary: Full re-validation of Vigilance Reports module (both Admin and Vigilance-User flows) after E1's blocker fix in /app/frontend/src/App.js (EmployeeRoute now contains a vigilance-designation bypass on /employee/vigilance, lines 88-110). Result: the prior 0%-reachable vigilance-user UI is now FULLY functional. Verified end-to-end: (a) madhan.s (onboarding REJECTED) and dinesh.t (onboarding approved) BOTH mount OperationalVigilance without onboarding redirect; (b) vig-own-table renders 25 base rows per page out of 50 total (vig-record-count: 'Showing 1–25 of 50 records'); (c) CRUD: edit dialog opens via vig-edit-btn (25 buttons, one per base row), Save persists 08:15:30 EXACTLY (verified via admin merged view: submissions[0].total_research_hours == '08:15:30', system_login '09:00 AM', system_logout '06:00 PM', total_break_hours '01:00'); (d) entry row in UI shows red trash icon (vig-delete-btn) AND pencil while pure-base rows show only the pencil; (e) Toast 'Entry created' appears on save; (f) DATA ISOLATION verified at both layers — dinesh GET /api/vigilance/entries returns 50 base rows with 0 rows-with-data after madhan's submit, AND backend PUT/DELETE of madhan's entry by dinesh→403 with messages 'You can only edit/delete your own vigilance entries'; (g) Admin merged view: GET /api/vigilance/entries as admin returns mode='admin' with per-row submissions[] array carrying uploaded_by_name + uploader-grouped fields (verified Adhitya Charan row); (h) Template download HTTP 200, 34KB xlsx for 01–06-Jun range; invalid range From>To returns HTTP 400 with the exact required message 'To Date must be the same as or after From Date.'; missing dates returns 422; (i) Export HTTP 200, 20KB xlsx; (j) Filter testids (vig-filter-employee/from/to/department/designation/team/btn), Upload (vig-upload-input), Add Entry, Download Template all present; (k) Non-vigilance user/pass123 still correctly blocked — sees [data-testid=vigilance-no-access], no vig-own-table. Cleanup performed: madhan's test entry deleted, admin merged view confirms 0 rows-with-submissions remain. NO STRAY DATA LEFT.

minor.issue: Returns 404 'Not Found'. The endpoint either doesn't exist or is named differently than docs/iter50 implied. Vigilance users currently rely on the main GET /api/vigilance/entries (which works) plus client-side filtering by submissions[].uploaded_by_employee_id. Not blocking, but adding /my-entries (or documenting its absence) would simplify own-entry queries.

issues: Re-check: README claims DialogDescription was added. Did not see Radix a11y warning in this run, so likely fixed — mark as RESOLVED pending E1 confirmation.

action_items: [LOW] Decide whether to expose GET /api/vigilance/my-entries for convenience, or remove any stale references to it from docs/tests — currently returns 404.
critical_code_review_comments: EmployeeRoute bypass (App.js:96-101) is correctly scoped: only triggers when (designation==='vigilance' && pathname startsWith /employee/vigilance). Good — minimum surface, won't accidentally leak access to other employee routes.

Backend ownership guard in vigilance router returns precise 403 messages on cross-user PUT/DELETE. Confirmed working. Recommend adding the same explicit check on bulk delete / upload-replace paths if any exist.

OperationalVigilance.js is still a single very large file (~1000+ lines). Splitting into EntryDialog.jsx, ViewDialog.jsx, useVigilanceData.js (hook), and filters/scroll utilities would improve maintainability and stay under the 700-line guideline.

success_rate.backend: 100% (all flows: access, CRUD, ownership-403, isolation, template, export, validation)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (vigilance-user base grid + CRUD + delete-button-conditional + non-vig block; admin merged-view data structure verified via API; UI render of submissions also confirmed in iter50)

test_credentials: admin/HrAdmin786$ (OK), madhan.s/Vigil@123 (OK — onboarding_status=rejected, designation=Vigilance, bypass works), dinesh.t/Vigil@123 (OK), user/pass123 (OK — correctly blocked).
seed_data_creation: Created 1 vigilance entry as madhan.s on 06-Jun-2026 against Adhitya Charan (id 01b7988e-b5e2-45bb-b2c4-7dca45e5da38) for the CRUD/isolation tests, then DELETED it via DELETE /api/vigilance/entries/{id} (200 ok). Final verification via admin GET /api/vigilance/entries?from=06-Jun-2026&to=06-Jun-2026 -> rows-with-submissions = 0. No stray data remains.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vigilance module is now in a fully working state. EmployeeRoute bypass at App.js:96-101 is the critical patch — if a future change reverts it, the vigilance-user UI will go dark again. Backend pytest is at 30/30 in test_vigilance_flow.py. The only minor open thread is whether GET /api/vigilance/my-entries should exist (currently 404). If you need to exercise multi-uploader admin merging via UI (not just API), have BOTH madhan.s and dinesh.t POST an entry for the SAME target+date, then login as admin and inspect /vigilance — you should see ONE merged row with both 'Madhan S' and 'Dinesh T' column groups under vig-admin-actions with per-uploader View/Edit/Delete (data structure already verified at API layer). Remember to DELETE any test entries created — 0 stray entries is a hard requirement from the user.
rca of the issue: Previous iteration_50 blocker (vigilance employees redirected to onboarding) is FIXED by the targeted bypass in EmployeeRoute (App.js lines 96-101): const isVigilanceEmployee = (user?.designation||'').toLowerCase()==='vigilance'; const onVigilancePath = location.pathname.startsWith('/employee/vigilance'); const allowVigilanceBypass = isVigilanceEmployee && onVigilancePath; … if (… needsOnboarding() && !allowVigilanceBypass) Navigate to onboarding. Combined with the backend change that exposes user.designation on /api/auth/me and gates onboarding on the user record rather than the document-derived onboarding record, this fully unblocks the vigilance UI for users whose onboarding has not been approved. Validated end-to-end with madhan.s (onboarding_status=rejected) successfully creating, editing, viewing, and deleting an entry via the UI.
